blocks.text: Document Name : Physical Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7)
Patient Name : KATHLEEN WHARTON Admission Date : 03/24/2026
Dx: L2 wedge fracture due to a GLF
Prec: goes by "Katie", fall risk, stroke in 12/25, not able to move RUE
Pt greeted supine in bed and agreeable to PT with no reports of
pain/dizziness at the start of session.
Quality Indicator: Roll left and right
Helper provided the following assistance to roll: Assistance with LESS
than or exactly half of all rolling tasks, including: Rolling to right from
back, Rolling to left from back
Patient is independent rolling R, min assist rolling L secondary to
residual weakness/hemiparesis R side from CVA 12-2025.
Roll left and right: Current Status
Helper provided the following assistance to move from sitting to lying:
Patient is CGA for BTB for upper/lower quarter, VC/TC for R UE/LE,
hemi-technique due to residual weakness from CVA UE>LE.
Quality Indicator: Lying to sitting on side of bed
Lying to sitting on side of bed
Lying to sitting on side of bed details
Helper provided the following assistance to move from lying to sitting:
Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Contact guard assistance
Lying to sitting on side of bed device(s)
Patient has an adjustable bed at home, CGA OOB L side with flat bed.
Lying to sitting on side of bed: Current

Patient is min assist for SPT from variety of surfaces using HW, cues
for R LE foot/alignment/positioning, weight-shifting, hand placement
on WC and correct placement of HW, pivoting, turning, BOS.

or van: Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Assistance with LESS than

and correct placement of HW, pivoting, turning, BOS.

Document Name : Physical Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7)
Patient Name : KATHLEEN WHARTON Admission Date : 03/24/2026
Quality Indicator: Walk 50 feet with two turns
Helper provided the following assistance for patient to walk 50 feet:
Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Contact guard assistance
Patient is able to ambulate X 50 feet level surfaces using HW with
CGA, VC/TC for BOS, sequencing, HW placement, performs step 2
patterning. Patient able to make multiple turns with CGA.
Helper provided the following assistance for patient to walk 150 feet:
Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Assistance with LESS than or
exactly half of all walking tasks, including: Trunk support
Patient is able to ambulate X 150 feet level surfaces using HW with
Min A, VC/TC for BOS, sequencing, HW placement, performs step 2
patterning. Patient able to make multiple turns with Min A. Patient
unsteady with increased distance due to LE fatigue R>L.
Quality Indicator: Walking 10 feet on uneven surfaces

Helper provided the following assistance for patient to walk 10 feet on
uneven surfaces: Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Assistance with
LESS than or exactly half of all walking tasks, including: Trunk support

Patient is able to ambulate X 10 feet unlevel surfaces/tumble mat
using HW VC/TC for BOS, sequencing, HW placement, performs step
2 patterning. Patient requires min assist for trunk/LE support due to
LE weakness R>L on uneven surfaces.
Walk 10 feet on uneven surfaces: Current
Walk 10 feet on uneven surfaces: Goal

assist for P/U object using reacher for steadying when transitioning

4 steps: Assistance with LESS than or exactly half of all step tasks,
Patient able to go up/down 4 stairs with one HR with min assist, assist
for R LE/placement/step length descending due to motor

is unable to go up and down 12 steps 6 stairs

for R LE/placement/step length descending due to motor
planning/control deficits from recent CVA. Patient limited to 6 stairs
Not attempted due to medical condition or safety concerns - 88
Does the patient use a wheelchair and/or
Assessed Weekly Qls with CGA/Min A d/t instability despite using
HW for stability on all mobility tasks. Pt reports increased fatigue post
Supervision by Tess Hofstra PT, DPT and Pt appropriate for PTA to
Document Name : Physical Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7)
Patient Name : KATHLEEN WHARTON Admission Date : 03/24/2026
Decreased endurance, Decreased strength, Impaired balance, Pain,
POC modified to best fit patient's current needs.
Continue bed mobility, transfers and gait
Treatment performed under CDC/facility guidelines for infection control. Pt ID confirmed by 2 identifiers prior to session.
Care transferred to nursing staff. Pt remains sitting up in WC, seat alarm in place, call light/telephone in reach, and
bedside table in front of pt. Pt reports this writer meets all their needs. (Daryl Sangco, PTA; 04/06/2026; 12:50; US)
GAIT TRAINING, 2 units; THER ACT DIRECT, 2 units
Document Name : Occupational Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7)
Patient Name : KATHLEEN WHARTON Admission Date : 03/24/2026
Pt declined to engage in further therapeutic interventions stating: "I'm
too depressed to do anything, I just don't want to be here anymore."
Upon arrival, pt found side-lying in bed w/ HOB raised. Pt not
agreeable to engage in therapeutic interventions and stated: "I'm too
depressed to do anything, I just don't want to be here anymore." Pt
also stated: "I walked around a lot with (PT) today, I don't need to do
Pt had no c/o pain throughout tx session.
Patient did not complete oral hygiene, Patient refused to complete
oral hygiene Therapist attempted, but pt declined.
Patient did not complete shower/bathing, Patient refused to complete
shower/bathing Therapist attempted, but pt declined.
Patient did not complete upper body dressing/undressing, Patient
refused to dress/undress upper body Therapist attempted, but pt
Patient did not complete lower body dressing/undressing, Patient
refused to dress/undress lower body Therapist attempted, but pt
Document Name : Occupational Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7)
Patient Name : KATHLEEN WHARTON Admission Date : 03/24/2026
Quality Indicator: Putting on/taking off footwear
Patient did not put on/take off footwear, Patient refused to put
on/take off footwear Therapist attempted, but pt declined.
Patient did not complete toileting hygiene, Patient refused to
complete toilet hygiene Therapist attempted, but pt declined.
Patient did not transfer on and off toilet or commode, Patient refused
to transfer on and off toilet or commode Therapist attempted, but pt
Needs reinforcement, continued education, or both
Therapist used good therapeutic use of self and empathetic listening
as pt voiced concerns of overall depression. Therapist provided max.
education and encouragement to attempt to engage in functional ADL
tasks and leisure activities to improve pts participation in functional
tasks necessary to maximize independence and improve overall QOL;
however, pt declined all attempted interventions despite therapists
max. encouragement and education on benefits and importance of
Pt not agreeable to engage in therapeutic interventions; therefore, tx
session ended early on this date. Therapist will attempt further
therapeutic interventions next tx session. Attempted tx session took
place in pts room. Pt left in comfortable position side-lying in bed w/
HOB raised, w/ all needs met and call light and tray table in reach.
Pt perseverating on d/c plans throughout attempted tx session and
therapist collaborated w/ CM. CM stated would follow up w/ pt.
Document Name : Occupational Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7)

60 min missed, low motivation, fatigue, depression. per patient.
pt. greeted in room and declined session. Patient stated "This stroke
has ruined me, i can't eat only honey, i can't move, can't hear, I'm
leaving tomororow, my assignment here has run out." multiple
attempts made and attempts at redirectioning however patient
Document Name : Physical Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7)
